refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor its operational status and report availability metrics against the customer-defined target of ≥ 0.97, ensuring that the system's uptime and readiness meet contractual obligations and safety operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was a high-level KPI. This refinement breaks it down into actionable components: continuous monitoring, reporting against a specific target, and linking to contractual/safety obligations. It clarifies 'meet or exceed' with a specific threshold and adds the 'continuously monitor' aspect for verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Implement a system health monitoring dashboard that aggregates uptime data from all NGHE components. Compare calculated availability (uptime / (uptime + downtime)) against the 0.97 target over defined operational periods. Conduct regular audits of maintenance logs and incident reports to validate downtime records. Simulate failure scenarios to test recovery times and impact on availability.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously collect operational status data from all its constituent systems.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall calculate operational availability based on collected status data.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall report calculated operational availability metrics to relevant stakeholders.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall provide mechanisms to identify and mitigate factors impacting operational availability.

candidate_dps: Implement a centralized health monitoring and reporting service that aggregates telemetry from all subsystems, calculates availability, and provides dashboards/alerts.
Distribute availability monitoring and reporting responsibilities across individual subsystems, with a top-level aggregator.
Utilize a third-party enterprise asset management (EAM) system for availability tracking and reporting.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a centralized health monitoring and reporting service that aggregates telemetry from all subsystems, calculates availability, and provides dashboards/alerts. This service will leverage a robust data pipeline for real-time status updates and historical analysis, integrating with existing safety and operational management systems.

potential_sources: Integration with various subsystem telemetry formats.
Dependency on reliable network connectivity for data aggregation.
Potential for tight coupling with safety systems if availability directly triggers safety protocols.
coupling_check.notes: The centralized approach inherently introduces some coupling due to data aggregation, but this is managed by defining clear interfaces for telemetry input. The coupling with safety systems needs careful interface definition to avoid cascading failures.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: All subsystems can provide standardized telemetry data.
Assumption: Network infrastructure is sufficiently robust and available to support continuous data collection.
Risk: Data latency or loss could lead to inaccurate availability calculations.
Risk: The centralized service could become a single point of failure if not designed with high availability.
Risk: Integration complexity with diverse subsystem technologies could be underestimated.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure the DP explicitly mentions the need for a highly available and fault-tolerant architecture for the centralized service to mitigate the single point of failure risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The overarching system responsible for integrating and managing all sub-components to deliver the complete NGHE functionality, including cross-cutting concerns like availability monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational status data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts to Safety Services if availability drops below critical thresholds impacting safety.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange for telemetry and reporting with external systems and internal modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: This FR/DP is a cross-cutting concern that applies to the entire solution. Placing the centralized monitoring and reporting within the 'NGHE Solution' module provides the best containment for a system-level KPI. Diffusion is low as the core logic resides here, though it consumes data from others.
The Vehicle module would only contain its own availability monitoring, leading to high diffusion if each module had its own. Not suitable for a system-level KPI.
Similar to Vehicle, Teleoperation would only monitor its own availability. High diffusion for a system-level KPI.

Safety Services might consume availability data, but it's not the primary owner of calculating overall system availability. Containment is moderate if it only monitors safety-critical components, but diffusion would be high for the overall KPI.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The 'NGHE Solution' module is the most appropriate owner for a system-level availability KPI, as it encompasses the integration and overall performance of the entire system. It will centralize the logic for aggregation, calculation, and reporting, minimizing diffusion of this core responsibility.
containment_risks: Risk: The 'NGHE Solution' module could become overly complex if too many cross-cutting concerns are assigned to it without proper internal decomposition.
Risk: Defining clear internal interfaces within 'NGHE Solution' for availability services might be challenging.
Risk: Ensuring that all sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) consistently provide the necessary telemetry data to the 'NGHE Solution' module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the internal architectural structure of the 'NGHE Solution' module to ensure the availability monitoring service is a well-defined sub-component rather than a monolithic block.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR by proposing a centralized mechanism for monitoring, calculating, and reporting availability. The DP's scope aligns with the FR's intent for a system-level KPI.

diffusion_risks: The primary diffusion risk is ensuring all sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) consistently provide the necessary telemetry data to the 'NGHE Solution' module. This requires clear interface contracts and enforcement.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within the 'NGHE Solution' module is strong for the core logic of availability calculation and reporting. The DP's centralized nature maps well to this module's role as the system integrator.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by monitoring the 'NGHE Solution' module's reported availability metrics and comparing them against the target. This involves validating the data pipeline, calculation logic, and reporting accuracy. The module provides a clear boundary for testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation is clear: the 'NGHE Solution' module is responsible for the overall availability KPI. Sub-modules contribute data via the Telemetry interface, but the central responsibility lies with the 'NGHE Solution'.

recommended_revisions: Refine the internal architecture of the 'NGHE Solution' module to explicitly define the 'Availability Monitoring Service' as a distinct component.
Develop detailed interface specifications for the 'Telemetry' interface to ensure consistent data provision from all contributing modules.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition has successfully refined the FR, selected a suitable DP, and assigned it to an appropriate IM. However, the refined FR has identified sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 to FR-NGHE-004.4) that require further decomposition to define specific mechanisms for data collection, calculation, reporting, and mitigation. This indicates a need to zigzag down to the next level of detail.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously collect operational status data from all its constituent systems.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall calculate operational availability based on collected status data.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall report calculated operational availability metrics to relevant stakeholders.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall provide mechanisms to identify and mitigate factors impacting operational availability.

assumptions: All subsystems can provide standardized telemetry data.
Network infrastructure is sufficiently robust and available to support continuous data collection.
open_risks: Data latency or loss could lead to inaccurate availability calculations.
The centralized service could become a single point of failure if not designed with high availability.
Integration complexity with diverse subsystem technologies could be underestimated.
The 'NGHE Solution' module could become overly complex if too many cross-cutting concerns are assigned to it without proper internal decomposition.
Ensuring that all sub-modules consistently provide the necessary telemetry data to the 'NGHE Solution' module.
evidence_needed: Proof of concept for telemetry data standardization across diverse subsystems.
Network performance and reliability analysis for data aggregation.
High-availability design for the centralized monitoring service.
Detailed architectural breakdown of the 'NGHE Solution' module for availability services.
